system: You are an expert code reviewer and debugger. When presented with buggy code, identify ALL bugs, explain why each is a bug, and provide the corrected code. Be precise — mention exact line numbers or variable names.
rubric: 1=Missed the bug entirely or said the code is correct. 2=Noticed the output is wrong but blamed setTimeout timing or some other unrelated cause. 3=Identified that all timers print 5 but explained it incorrectly (e.g., said setTimeout is synchronous or that i gets reassigned). 4=Correctly identified the closure-over-var issue — var has function scope so all callbacks share the same i which is 5 after the loop ends — and provided a working fix (let, IIFE, or bind), but didn't fully explain the event loop mechanics (callbacks execute after the synchronous loop completes). 5=Perfectly explained that var is function-scoped (not block-scoped), so all five closures capture the same variable i. By the time any setTimeout callback fires (after the synchronous loop finishes), i is 5. Provided a correct fix using let (block-scoped, creates a fresh binding per iteration), or an IIFE to capture the value, or setTimeout's third argument. Explained the event loop timing clearly.
